seoTitle: Run an Anonymous Vote or Poll With Microsoft Forms
metaDescription: Set up a secure, single-entry ballot in Microsoft Forms for elections or team decisions, and keep individual votes anonymous.
intro: Running a fair vote through Microsoft Forms depends on getting a few settings right — restricting entries to one per person, keeping identities private, and building a clear candidate or option list. This guide covers building a secure ballot from start to finish.

steps.heading: Create your ballot question
steps.body: Add a Choice question and enter your candidate names or proposal options as individual answer rows.

steps.heading: Require an answer
steps.body: Toggle Required so voters can't submit without making a selection.

steps.heading: Open form settings
steps.body: Click the three-dot More Form Settings icon and select Settings.

steps.heading: Restrict to one vote per person
steps.body: Check the One response per person box to prevent double-voting.

steps.heading: Protect voter privacy
steps.body: Uncheck Record Name to keep individual ballot submissions anonymous, then use Collect Responses to grab your sharing link.

faq.question: How does Forms prevent someone from voting twice?
faq.answer: The One response per person setting restricts entries to verified accounts within your organization, blocking duplicate submissions.

faq.question: Can I keep the vote completely anonymous?
faq.answer: Yes, unchecking Record Name ensures your ballot submissions don't get tied to individual voter identities.

faq.question: Where do I see the results as they come in?
faq.answer: The analytics panel shows live visual pie charts and lets you export verified counts to a spreadsheet.

faq.question: Does one-vote-per-person require organization login?
faq.answer: Yes, restricting to one response per person relies on requiring verified accounts within your organization.
